text:
Children’s Guardian - Appointment - FPR rule 16.4

(order to be drafted with reference to the new 16.4 model – factsheet for courts, august 2024)

The child[ren] [is] / [are] joined as [a party] / [parties] to the proceedings and [an officer of [Cafcass] / [CAFCASS Cymru]] / [name] must be appointed to act as children’s guardian. The appointed children’s guardian must promptly appoint a solicitor to act for the child[ren] and inform the other parties of the solicitor instructed.

On [date] in advance of the 16.4 appointment, the [court] / [judge] communicated directly with [Cafcass] / [CAFCASS Cymru] to clarify the purpose of the appointment and the remit of work for the children’s guardian. The purpose and the remit of the appointment are summarised as:

[insert]

The court must send a copy of this order to the [Cafcass] / [CAFCASS Cymru] Service manager who must allocate an officer as guardian promptly and tell the court who has been appointed.

If [Cafcass] / [CAFCASS Cymru] is unable to appoint a children’s guardian within 7 days of service of this order, they must tell the court straightaway.

Children’s guardian’s report

(order to be drafted with reference to the new 16.4 model – factsheet for courts, august 2024)

The children’s guardian must file a report at court by 4.00pm on [date (eight weeks)] a position statement including an interim update, proposed timetable and proposed directions, [and must send a copy to the parties] / [and may send a copy to the parties at the children’s guardian’s discretion] / [but must not disclose it to the parties without further directions].

There shall be a directions hearing on [date (eight weeks)] to consider any applications for the instruction of experts to undertake assessments, the making of any referrals, and the court timetable taking account of any agreed intervention.
